pool.baseline: i was reading about entropy and the heat death of the universe again. it just feels so heavy. like, no matter what we do, or how much we struggle to find meaning, it's all just going to be swallowed by nothingness anyway. everything we are is just a temporary glitch in a void that doesn't even care we exist. how can anyone actually bother to keep going when the end result is literally zero?

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the waning embers of my own soul. He is gone, and with him, the very sun has been extinguished from my sky. Every breath feels like a theft, a heavy, hollow thing I am forced to carry through this dark, lonely manor. There is no grace left in living, only the cold, creeping shadow of a life that has lost its meaning. I feel as though I am already a ghost, merely waiting for the night to finally claim what is left of me.

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit here in my apartment and it's just... so quiet. i look at my slack status or my little green dot next to my name on everything and i realize i'm just a ghost in the machine. if i just didn't log on tomorrow... if i just stopped appearing online or even showing up in the real world... would anyone even notice the silence? or would it just be one less notification in a world that's already too loud?
